title: The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
subhead: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.

France unveils plan to ditch all fossil fuels by 2050
subhead: Dozens of nations gathered in Colombia to hold the first-ever talks on how to transition away from fossil fuels. France published a 'roadmap' that explicitly aims to phase out coal by 2030, oil by 2045 and gas for energy purposes by 2050.

title: As Mali's junta struggles, Algeria regains some of its influence in the Sahel
subhead: The setbacks the Malian junta has suffered against insurgent groups have reestablished Algiers as a mediator, after years of waning influence in the region.

title: 'Severance' star Adam Scott still feels he is 'scrounging for acceptance'
subhead: After breaking out with 'Parks and Recreation,' the 53-year-old American actor has established himself as a major figure on television. At Canneseries, where he gave a master class, he received the Canal+ Icon Award from the festival.

title: 'The benefits of music are immediate, varied and free of side effects'
subhead: In an interview, Isabelle Peretz, a neuropsychologist who researches the neurocognition of music explains the effects of songs and sounds on patients with Alzheimer's or Parkinson's disease, as well as on premature infants.

subhead: Implemented gradually since November 2025 due to the risk of drowning, the new policy has so far resulted in seven interceptions, according to a Le Monde count.

title: As bird numbers plummet, Brittany's seagull egg sterilization is facing mounting criticism
subhead: The sterilization measure was implemented to manage the population of this emblematic seabird, but is now being applied less and less due to questions over its effectiveness. Meanwhile, the number of seagulls has dropped significantly.

title: Bologna, a symbol of progressive Italy, is targeted by the far right
subhead: Heir to humanist ideas and an antifascist culture established after World War II, the capital of Emilia-Romagna has become a laboratory for resistance to reactionary ideas in Italy.
